mechanism_narrative: Persistently elevated year-over-year consumer price inflation signals strong nominal demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward test shows months with above-median CPIAUCSL inflation do not produce positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor income and falling household consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously elevated federal funds rates and month-over-month industrial production declines generate a financial conditions squeeze that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-INDPRO interaction adds no incremental directional accuracy over a single-factor FEDFUNDS model over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.0%, month-over-month declines in retail sales signal acute demand destruction that amplifies earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, negative RSAFS surprises fail to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production indicates improving capacity utilization and operating leverage, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month changes in INDPRO do not coincide with positive 5-day SPX returns in more than 52% of weeks.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth combined with contained consumer price inflation creates a real growth environment that sustains corporate earnings without provoking monetary tightening, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-CPIAUCSL interaction does not outperform a single-factor PAYEMS model in directional accuracy over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly housing starts falling below 1,200 thousand units signal contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Triggered instances with HOUST below 1,200 produce negative 5-day returns in fewer than 55% of cases over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive quarterly declines in real GDP trigger expectations of an earnings recession that drive negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: Episodes with two consecutive quarterly GDPC1 declines fail to produce negative 60-day returns in more than 60% of instances.
